review: STAY AWAY!If I could give zero stars I would.They offer you a good market value, then they drag you along, and then they offer you less than 50% of their initial offer, which is 30% of actual value.THEY ARE UNETHICAL!

You can't post a zero or negative number, so a 1 will have to do.I have received multiple text messages from this "company", from various phone numbers with (360) area code, all asking about the same property at 4802 N. Foxglove Dr NW. in Bellingham WA. I don't even live in WA state, nor have I purchased any property other than the home I live in now. I have filed a complaint with BBB but they can't really do anything. I keep blocking their number, but another one pops up from them. While my complaint was falling on deaf ears, I got another one yesterday. They're like a nasty VD. Every response from them is like all the other complaints "sorry for the inconvenience". I wanted to know how they got my contact information tagged to that property so I can chase down the source and correct the error. Instead of working with me, you apparently could care less.Khang, you already have my contact details and are clearly unwilling to comply with my simple request. You need to stop using deceitful business practices, like using untraceable numbers, and take my F'ing number out of your system. Here's a tip...if the number you're trying to contact ISN'T a 360 area code, how about not including it in your "Marketing Department" contact list. I've been on the FTC site since 2003, but you obviously don't care. "We promise to treat you with respect because that is how we would want to be treated." - too bad you really don't live by your own motto. Maybe this post will make you take action. From your history of complaints on BBB and those listed here...I would bet you don't do a damn thing.
Unethical vultures. They continue to call, text, and send post cards despite repeated requests to stop. I am on the FTC Do Not Call list, and they repeatedly violate it.

Two calls last week and this afternoon alone, received 2 phone calls and a text from someone at this "company" leaving messages stating that they wanted to speak to me about my home. Home is not for sale, there is no interest in selling anytime soon and have ZERO interest in doing any business with them. Continued harassment will result in complaints being filed with local and state officials.

Irresponsible advertising techniques used to scrounge up business!!Their "3rd Notice" postcard regarding "the property at (my home address) due to some important things that have come up with the property" concerned my teen son, (who had picked up the mail early in the morning), enough to wake me up, worried we were going to lose our home or had a serious urgent property issue happening. More wording on the card stressed the importance of not delaying and multiple attempts to reach me.Nowhere in the postcard was mentioned from which agency or company this "3rd Notice" on a bright yellow postcard was - just "Alex", a phone number and PO Box in Bham. Called and it turns out they are a buy-your-house-for-cash business. "Alex" was a woman's voice on an answering machine.No thank you - I will use a bonafide Realtor (held to a level of professional ethics) when it's time to sell my home. You don't need to stoop low and use slimey scare-tactic advertising techniques to drum up business. I'll give my business to someone honorable who actually works for it. Quit bothering and worrying people unnecessarily.
ridiculous and annoying having to block 5 different calling phone numbers.just calls no mail yet--thank ya jesus

businessResponse.comment: Hi Nancy, Sorry for the mailer we sent ya. We were looking to buy some houses/property in the area so thought that we sent you one to see if you are interested in selling as we are not sure who want to sell or not. We are sorry for the inconvenience. Sorry and Thank you.

review: Someone from this place keeps sending me mail saying he wants to buy my house. I am not selling my house and I am tired of his annoying junk mail.
We keep getting these urgent notices saying it is important for us to call them. We are not selling our house, especially for pennies on the dollar. Tired of getting these. I want them to stop.
